chief_complaint: A 64-year-old woman presented with symptoms of heart failure and worsening dyspnea.
history: The patient had a history of stage IV squamous cell carcinoma of the right lung and had undergone chemotherapy with carboplatin, paclitaxel, and pembrolizumab 2 weeks prior to presentation.
exam: Vital signs: not provided. Physical examination findings: not provided.
labs: Electrocardiography (EKG) revealed right bundle branch block with ST elevations. Troponin peaked at 424 ng/L, and proBNP 865 pg/mL. Transthoracic echocardiogram (TTE) showed left ventricle ejection fraction (LVEF) of 35% to 39% along with akinesis of all the mid- to apical left ventricle (LV) wall segments.
correct_diagnosis: Takotsubo Syndrome Triggered by Immune Checkpoint Inhibitor-Induced Pneumonitis
differentials: Takotsubo Syndrome Triggered by Immune Checkpoint Inhibitor-Induced Pneumonitis
correct_ranking: Takotsubo Syndrome Triggered by Immune Checkpoint Inhibitor-Induced Pneumonitis
